div1.entryFree:
Scour, 1) to rub hard: the chairs of order s. with juice of balm, Wiv. V, 5, 65. to be --ed to nothing with perpetual motion, H4B I, 2, 246.
2) to rub for the purpose of cleaning, to cleanse; absol.: she can wash and s. Gent. III, 1, 313. Wiv. I, 4, 101. trans.: Gent. III, 1, 315. H5 II, 1, 60. H6B I, 3, 195. III, 2, 199. Per. II, 2, 55.
3) to remove for the purpose of cleaning, to sweep away, to purge: which (blood), washed away, shall s. my shame with it, H4A III, 2, 137. never came reformation in a flood, with such a heady currance, --ing faults, H5 I, 1, 34. what rhubarb would s. these English hence? Mcb. V, 3, 56.
